title: quercus lanuginosa var. suberoides
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. suberoides, also known as the Quaking Red Oak, is a deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. Its bark is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Quercus lanuginosa var. suberoides can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
